rule_name: Never correct known technical whitelist terms
notes: Preserve original spelling and capitalization for known Apple/API/UI/dev terms.

rule_name: Never correct mixedCase / camelCase / PascalCase tokens
notes: Examples: SwiftUI, CoreML, URLSession, ViewController, asyncSequence.
Examples: API, SDK, DNS, VPN, HTTP, JSON, ML, AI.

rule_name: Never correct tokens with dots/slashes/underscores/hyphens used as technical delimiters
notes: Examples: Node.js, HTTP/3, .plist, en_US, com.app.bundle, CI/CD.

rule_name: Never correct bundle identifiers / reverse DNS identifiers
Never correct semantic versions and build-like tokens
Never correct tokens with digits plus letters
Never correct command-line flags and options
Never correct URLs, emails, handles and hashtags
notes: Technical and identity tokens should not be layout-corrected.

rule_name: Never auto-correct short unknown words before checking technical guards
pattern: length <= 4 and matches any guard above
notes: Prevents damage to short acronyms like DNS, VPN, API, CLI, ML.

rule_name: Correct only after whitelist/rule guard fails
notes: Run layout candidate scoring only after technical protection rules are checked.
